_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of common forensics tool configuration and support applications (e.g., VMWare, WIRESHARK).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.458), DA-K-004 (DA, cosine: 0.458).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.7/10 (raw 0.274). Shared vocabulary: [common, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [applications, configuration, forensics, support, tool, vmware] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, data, engineering, file, fundamentals, line] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.854 (P=0.850, R=0.858). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 32-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (68.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of common forensics tool configuration and support applications (e.g.," and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.471 > STS 0.274 (gap +0.196). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [common, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.8%. Shared tokens: [common, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [applications, configuration, forensics, support, tool, vmware]. WIDAI-only: [command, data, engineering, file, fundamentals, line].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 68.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.196 with low STS 0.274. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
